message:
So last week the power went out for like six hours, and I realized I'd become *that person* — the one who talks about being prepared but whose flashlights are... somewhere? In a junk drawer? With dead batteries? 😅

I ended up using my phone's flashlight (battery dying), squinting at the fridge every 10 minutes to see what I could grab, and basically sitting in the dark feeling very smug about how *ready* I am.

Turns out "ready" and "actually set up" are two different things. Funny how that works.

Now I've got a little system: cheap LED lantern in the kitchen, a basic power bank by my bed, flashlights actually *together* in one spot with fresh batteries labeled with the date. Not fancy. Just... there when I need them.

The best part? No scrambling in the dark, no dead phone anxiety, no pretending I have it all figured out.

Do you have a go-to light source you keep handy, or are you still in the "someday I'll organize that" phase like I was?
